_about: Josh Braun-specific machine-readable rules. Layered on top of cold-email-benchmarks.json (the cross-source baseline). Channel-specific length tables, JB-only banned phrases, the leading-question regex, and the illumination-question heuristic.

cold_email_followup_3plus._source: Pattern interrupts: lumpy mail, video, embedded graphic

linkedin_connection_request._source: LinkedIn free-tier 200-char ceiling; JB DM example is 32 words / 200 chars

linkedin_dm_post_connect._source: JB cadence; LinkedIn 1300-char ceiling allows more but JB stays short

post_event_followup._source: Post-event "It was great meeting at X" pattern

_median_sentence_length_source: LinkedIn-CSV stats: median sentence = 6 words; 78% of sentences under 10. Soft cap at 12.
_additional_banned_phrases_source: JB CSV phrase-frequency analysis (data/josh-braun-frameworks.md:380-401). Counts in 1998-post corpus: best-in-class 0, world-class 0, cutting-edge 0, industry-leading 0, robust 0, scalable 0, drive results 0, game-changer 0. JB ridicules "if I could 10x your revenue" + "would you be interested" (4T:47-62).

leading_question_pattern.regex: \b(if I could|wouldn'?t you|don'?t you think|would you be interested|would you be open to a (?:15|30)|would you agree)\b

illumination_question_required._source: 4T:84-100. Every cold-email-first-touch and post-connect DM must have at least one neutral how/what/why-are-you question.
